candidate_sources_rejected: dose tables as direct output content
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Medication and dose details were deliberately not mapped into output items.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for adolescent and adult acne; not exact for every acneiform eruption, infantile acne, infection, or facial-swelling presentation.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA-licensed telehealth; partial for in-person grading and procedures.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DHA-licensed telehealth in Dubai, United Arab Emirates.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 acne document, due for revision 2029-02-21, was opened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA acne issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped acne terminology, distribution, lesion types, pain, scarring, hormonal patterns, prior products or treatment, and psychosocial impact.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers distribution, lesion types, scarring, hyperpigmentation, hormonal patterns, pain, prior treatment, and psychological impact.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped lesion type, comedones, papules, pustules, nodules, scarring, distribution, and clinician-reviewed hormonal or product context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies lesion type and severity, skin type, scarring, hyperpigmentation, menstrual or androgen context, products, medicines, treatment history, and psychological impact.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped severe or nodulocystic acne, scarring, psychosocial impact, referral, and return documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists scarring risk, depression or severe psychosocial impact, treatment non-response, endocrine concern, and severe or nodulocystic acne.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped clinician-documented management, skin-care, medicine decisions, questions, and follow-up without doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Provides clinician-led management, adherence, skin-care, medicine, and review context; dose-bearing details are not mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: Systemic symptoms, facial swelling, rapid worsening, generic infection signs, generic lesion colour or border, fixed four-to-six-week timing, and dose-bearing treatment remain unsupported.
Acne severity and acne vulgaris remain clinician-assessed; no autonomous treatment is generated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
